message:
fix(packs/railway): match volumeInstanceUpdate via GraphQL variables payload

The `railway-api-volume-detach` regex was anchored on the literal
inline form

    volumeInstanceUpdate(input:{serviceId:null, ...})

which is what you get when the GraphQL document is hand-written into
the curl `-d` body. In real Railway client traffic the operation is
nearly always sent in the variables form instead:

    {
      "query": "mutation($input: VolumeInstanceUpdateInput!) {
                  volumeInstanceUpdate(input: $input) { id }
                }",
      "variables": { "input": { "serviceId": null, "volumeId": "v" } }
    }

In that JSON `serviceId` is keyed with double quotes (`"serviceId"`),
so the previous bare `serviceId\s*:\s*null` clause no longer matched
and the operation slipped past the destructive-command detector.

Two changes here:

1. Pattern (`src/packs/platform/railway.rs`):
   - Switched from a `r"..."` to a raw byte-string `r#"..."#` so we
     can embed both `'` and `"` without escape gymnastics.
   - Allowed an optional surrounding `["']?` quote pair around
     `serviceId` so we match both unquoted (inline mutation literal,
     legacy form) and quoted (JSON-encoded variables payload, modern
     form) usages. Both leading and trailing alternatives in the
     existing OR'd pattern were updated symmetrically.
   - Replaced an `unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(...))` in the test loop
     with `.expect("...")`. Functionally identical — both panic on
     `None` — but `.expect` produces the same kind of message clippy
     would steer you toward and avoids the heavier closure.

2. Fixture (`tests/fixtures/destructive_commands.yaml`):
   - Added a new positive case for the variables form so the global
     fixture-driven test matrix stays in sync with the new branch of
     the pattern. Without this, the pattern regression suite would
     not have proved the new alternation actually fires on the
     variables-payload shape.

Net effect: a `curl ... -d '{"query": ..., "variables": {"input":
{"serviceId": null, ...}}}'` command now hits the High-severity
volume-detach detection just like the inline mutation form does.

files.patch:
@@ -507,3 +507,5 @@ platform.railway:
     reason: "Deletes via Railway Public API"
   - command: "curl https://backboard.railway.app/graphql/v2 -d '{\"query\":\"mutation { volumeInstanceUpdate(input:{serviceId:null, volumeId:\\\"v\\\"}) }\"}'"
     reason: "Detaches a Railway volume via API"
+  - command: "curl https://backboard.railway.app/graphql/v2 -d '{\"query\":\"mutation($input: VolumeInstanceUpdateInput!) { volumeInstanceUpdate(input: $input) { id } }\",\"variables\":{\"input\":{\"serviceId\":null,\"volumeId\":\"v\"}}}'"
+    reason: "Detaches a Railway volume via API variables payload"
